topic: Lactase production in pigs and rats
image_prompt: SVG: line graph, Lactase production (y, arrow axis, no numeric scale) vs Days after birth (x, arrow axis, no numeric scale). Two curves: Pig (orange) starts high and declines then plateaus at a moderate level; Rat (teal) starts lower, declines and plateaus at a low level; Pig stays above Rat throughout. Curve labels Pig and Rat at right ends.

topic: Fibre stretch vs sodium hydroxide concentration
image_prompt: SVG: line graph, Increase in length at break / mm (y, 0 to 4.5, ticks every 0.5) vs Concentration of sodium hydroxide / % (x, 0 to 35, ticks every 5). Points joined by line: (0,2.5),(1,3.3),(2,4.2),(5,3.7),(10,2.3),(15,2.6),(20,2.4),(25,2.5),(30,2.2). Rises to a peak near 2% then falls and stays roughly level.

topic: Elbow joint — antagonistic muscles X and Y
image_prompt: SVG: two panels of the elbow joint. Left: arm extended, biceps (Muscle X) relaxed/elongated and triceps (Muscle Y) visible. Right: arm flexed/raised, biceps (Muscle X) contracted/bulged and triceps (Muscle Y) elongated. Labels Muscle X and Muscle Y in both panels; subtle skin/bone gradients.

topic: Active vs standard packaging — banana ripening
image_prompt: SVG: four-panel diagram. Top row Standard packaging: bananas + apple before (yellow) → after time (brown spotted bananas, orange apple) with grey gas dots labelled 'Gas'. Bottom row Active packaging: same fruits but inside box surrounded by red dots (gas absorbers) and orange coating border; gas being pulled to edges via arrows; after time fruits remain fresh. Key bottom-left: red dot = Gas absorber, orange line = Coating to prevent growth of microbes, grey dot = Gas produced by ripening fruit.

topic: Bacteria micrographs — fresh vs salted meat
image_prompt: SVG: two side-by-side black-background microscope-style images. Left 'Fresh meat': smooth plump oval bacterium (light cream) on dark tissue background. Right 'Salted meat': clearly shrunken irregular bacterium with rough wrinkled outline, smaller area, dark dimples for collapsed cytoplasm, thin pale salt-crystal streaks radiating outward.

SVG: simplified world map. Most continents in light grey. Highlighted in red: Finland (small in northern Europe), Brazil (South America), China (East Asia), Australia, and a small southern African country — indicating high lactose tolerance / high milk consumption regions. Legend at bottom: red = High lactose tolerance / high milk consumption, grey = other countries.

topic: Agave plant, drying fibres, woven product triptych
image_prompt: SVG triptych. Panel 1: agave plant with radial spiked succulent leaves on red soil under blue sky. Panel 2: wooden frame racks with bundles of fibres hanging vertically to dry, on green grass. Panel 3: woven brown basket with weave pattern and handle on cream background. Caption beneath: agave plants → drying fibres → finished woven product.

SVG: equipment set-up. Left column: two glass humidity chambers labelled '0% humidity' and '25% humidity' with fibre coil inside; 25% chamber shows water droplets in air. Middle: black square with white right-arrow (interactive cue). Right: retort stand on a blue base, vertical steel rod, horizontal cross-rod with clamp holding a hanging plant fibre, mass holder hanging below, three 10 g labelled disc masses stacked nearby. Labels: clamp, plant fibre, mass holder.

SVG: four stacked glass humidity chambers labelled 0%, 25%, 50%, 75% humidity, each with a fibre coil and increasing density of water droplets in the air. Right side: same retort stand with clamp, fibre, mass holder, three 10 g masses.

topic: Data table — Humidity vs Mass to break (Group 1 & 2)
image_prompt: SVG data table: columns Humidity / %, Mass needed to break the plant fibre / g split into Group 1 and Group 2. Rows: 0 / 30 / 27.5; 25 / 40 / 32.5; 50 / 40 / 37.5. Beneath: Draggable items and key — Group 1 red diamond, Group 2 blue circle.

topic: Plant cell micrographs at 25% vs 75% humidity + scale
image_prompt: SVG: two circular microscope views. Left '25% Humidity': many small densely-packed plant cells, one highlighted (darker outline) with a double-headed arrow labelled '80 micrometres'. Right '75% Humidity': fewer larger cells with visible gaps between them, one highlighted cell larger; labels 'Gaps between cells' and 'Cell wall' on right with leader lines. Beneath: scale ruler bar 0–400 micrometres in 50-unit major ticks with minor 10-unit ticks, labelled 'micrometres'.

topic: Flow chart — NaOH treatment of plant fibres
image_prompt: SVG horizontal flow chart with four stages and arrows: (1) bundle of curly orange plant fibres labelled 'Plant fibres of the same diameter and length' → arrow 'Immersion' → (2) glass beaker with blue NaOH solution and fibres inside, labelled 'Sodium hydroxide solution' → arrow 'Washing' → (3) straightened wet fibres with water droplets dripping → arrow 'Drying' → (4) dried straight fibres labelled 'Plant fibre treated with sodium hydroxide solution'.

SVG: isometric biosolar roof scene. Green grass roof with trees (one bearing yellow fruits), small flowering bushes, and three angled blue solar panels on metal legs. Adjacent multi-storey building with windows on right. Three white label boxes: 'Plants', 'Soil layer', 'Solar panels'.

SVG: same biosolar roof scene. 'Soil layer' label rendered as dark highlighted box with hover indicator; adjacent expanded info box with bulleted text: supports plant growth; provides insulation to the building; absorbs water to reduce flooding; reduces pollution from rainwater runoff. 'Plants' and 'Solar panels' labels remain plain.

SVG: same biosolar roof scene. 'Plants' label rendered as dark highlighted box with hover indicator; adjacent expanded info box with bulleted text: remove pollution from air; have a cooling effect; can be grown to produce food; provide green spaces to improve wellbeing of people; increase urban biodiversity. 'Soil layer' and 'Solar panels' labels remain plain.
